reasoning: Recent data from FRED shows the 10Y-2Y treasury spread experiencing downward momentum, dropping from 0.74 on Feb 5 to 0.59 on Feb 27. This short-term momentum increases the likelihood that early March spread values will remain depressed or continue to fall, resulting in a slightly lower-than-even probability of an increase by March 8. For the March 31 horizon, while short-term localized trends matter less and the longer-term structural trend since late 2025 has been curve steepening, the overall uncertainty increases, moving the expectation closer to the baseline base rate of 50%.
